macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: US Jobs Report Disappoints: Only 57,000 Jobs Added in June, May Revised Down
items.summary: The US economy added just 57,000 jobs in June, barely half the 114,000 expected, while May's job numbers were quietly revised down by another 43,000, signaling a weakening labor market.
items.source: YouTube - The Fed Just Blinked and Bitcoin Noticed

items.title: Fed Rate Hike Odds Collapse from 62% to 47% Following Weak Jobs Data
items.summary: Rate hike odds on Polymarket fell from 62% to 47% after the weaker-than-expected June jobs report, with expectations likely to decline further as labor market conditions soften.
items.source: YouTube - The Fed Just Blinked and Bitcoin Noticed

items.title: Bitcoin Surges 3.5% to $62,000 as Crypto Market Cap Gains $69 Billion
items.summary: Bitcoin reclaimed $62,000, rising 3.5% and adding roughly $41 billion to its market cap, while Ethereum surged 6.9%, contributing to a $69 billion increase in total crypto market cap over 6 hours.
items.source: YouTube - The Fed Just Blinked and Bitcoin Noticed

items.title: India and Japan Sign Sweeping AI, Defense, and Economic Security Pact Worth 10 Trillion Yen
items.summary: India and Japan unveiled agreements covering AI, defense technology, critical minerals, and energy, including their first defense co-development deal for the naval radio antenna 'unicorn' project and a goal to attract 10 trillion yen in Japanese investment.
items.source: YouTube - Modi and Japan PM Sign AI and Defence Pact Worth 10 Trillion Yen

items.title: Ampol Reports EV Uptake at 18% of New Car Sales in Q2, Double Last Year's 8%
items.summary: Ampol noted a structural shift away from petrol, with EVs accounting for over 18% of new car sales in Q2 2026 compared to 8% in 2025, while diesel prices remain above pre-war levels despite petrol returning to normal.
items.source: YouTube - Ampol's big push into EV charging

items.title: Germany Plans to Tighten Sick Leave Rules to Boost Productivity and Competitiveness
items.summary: Germany's government is introducing stricter sick leave regulations as part of broader economic reforms aimed at enhancing worker productivity and national competitiveness.
items.source: Instagram - Germany's government plans to tighten sick leave rules

items.title: Companies Shift from 'Tokenmaxxing' to Efficient AI Spending After Rising Token Bills
items.summary: Corporate policy on 'tokenmaxxing'—excessively using AI models to inflate internal leaderboards—has peaked and declined as companies adopt more efficient AI spending strategies following high token costs.
items.source: Yahoo Finance - Tokenmaxxing is out, but companies are still spending on AI

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Surges Above $61K as Market Cap Climbs to $2.12T
items.summary: Bitcoin rose to $61,292 (+0.80% in 24h) while Ethereum gained 4.22% to $1,698 and Solana jumped nearly 18% to $81, lifting total crypto market capitalization to $2.12 trillion despite Fear & Greed Index remaining in 'fear' at 22.

items.title: BlackRock Transfers Over 20,000 BTC to Coinbase in Institutional Flow Signal
items.summary: BlackRock moved more than 20,000 BTC to Coinbase across four consecutive days, signaling large institutional inflows into a centralized exchange that could support crypto adoption and liquidity amid mixed US macro data.

items.title: $184 Million in Crypto Liquidations Reflect Market Volatility
items.summary: The crypto market experienced significant volatility with $184 million in liquidations over 24 hours, including $88.19 million in long positions and $96.10 million in short positions, impacting both bullish and bearish traders.

items.title: Nasdaq and Kraken Partner to Launch Tokenized Stock Trading
items.summary: Nasdaq and Kraken announced a partnership to launch trading for tokenized stocks, while analysts note the US's limited exposure to oil shocks may be supporting Bitcoin's price stability.

items.title: Aon Tests Stablecoin Payments for Insurance Premiums with Coinbase
items.summary: Global insurance broker Aon is piloting stablecoin payments for insurance premiums in a test with Coinbase and Paxos, joining PayPal and Stripe in adding stablecoin support despite Jack Dorsey's long-held Bitcoin-only stance.

items.title: US Jobs Data Misses Forecasts as Unemployment Falls to Yearly Low
items.summary: US June payrolls missed forecasts while unemployment dropped to 4.2%, a yearly low, adding macro uncertainty to crypto markets despite Bitcoin's rebound above $60K.

items.title: Kalshi and Polymarket Seek $20B Valuations in Fundraising Talks
items.summary: Crypto prediction platforms Kalshi and Polymarket are reportedly seeking $20 billion valuations in new fundraising talks, highlighting growing institutional interest in crypto derivatives markets.

items.title: XRP Builds Higher Base Above $1.08 After Selloff
items.summary: XRP established a higher trading base above $1.08 following last week's selloff, though bulls need a clean break above $1.10 to confirm momentum is shifting, while Cardano climbed 11% in a green day.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Flipkart Deploys 250+ AI Models as 40% of Code Becomes AI-Written
items.summary: Flipkart has built its own specialized large language models for e-commerce, deploying over 250 AI models across its ecosystem, with AI now writing 35–40% of its software code, signaling a major shift in retail backend development.

items.title: Amazon Announces $13 Billion AI and Cloud Investment in India Through 2030
items.summary: Amazon revealed a staggering $13 billion investment in India to expand cloud and AI infrastructure, pushing its total planned investment in the country to $48 billion, intensifying the AI arms race in Indian e-commerce.

items.title: Meesho Reports Over 70% of Software Code Is Now AI-Generated
items.summary: Rival e-commerce platform Meesho revealed that more than 70% of its software code is entirely AI-generated, accelerating the shift from experimental AI to production-scale deployment in India's retail sector.

items.title: Claude Fable 5 Leaked System Prompt Emerges Online
items.summary: Claude Fable 5 has returned, and its entire system prompt was leaked online shortly after release, sparking interest in the model's capabilities and internal architecture.

items.title: Apple Unveils New Siri AI Updates at WWDC 2026
items.summary: Apple introduced significant Siri AI enhancements at WWDC 2026, marking Tim Cook's final WWDC and advancing on-device AI integration for iOS users.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Sony Faces Industry Pushback Over End of Physical Games as Star Fox Switch 2 Sales Top Charts
items.summary: Sony announced the end of physical game discs, sparking widespread backlash from game companies and fans, while Star Fox on the Switch 2 debuted with 41,680 copies sold in Japan, topping the sales chart.

items.title: Rockstar Responds to GTA 6 Physical Edition Backlash with Promise of True Disc Version Post-Launch
items.summary: Rockstar Games addressed gamer outrage over GTA 6's digital-only release by confirming a true disc version will be released shortly after launch, though current physical editions will only include code.

items.title: Xbox Obsidian Studio Closure Rumors Retracted as Reports Confirm Studio is Safe
items.summary: Initial reports suggesting Microsoft planned to shut down Obsidian Entertainment were retracted by multiple sources including Windows Central and gamebusiness.com, confirming the studio remains operational.

items.title: Capcom Announces Onimusha Release Date Shift to September 4, 2025
items.summary: Capcom revealed that Onimusha will be released earlier than expected, moving its launch date to September 4, 2025, following an earlier leak confirming a September release.

items.title: GTA 6 Generates $3 Billion in 24 Hours Despite Global Bans
items.summary: GTA 6 achieved a mind-blowing $3 billion in revenue within its first 24 hours, overcoming massive regional bans and setting a new milestone for video game launches.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Tesla Launches Model Y Long Wheelbase in US with Six Seats at $62,000
items.summary: Tesla has begun taking orders for the Model Y Long Wheelbase in the US and Puerto Rico, featuring six seats and a price tag of $62,000.

items.title: Form Energy Secures $405M to Accelerate 100-Hour Battery Development
items.summary: Form Energy, a company producing longer-lasting alternatives to lithium batteries, announced $405 million in funding to speed development of its 100-hour battery technology.

items.title: Amazon Leo Satellite Broadband Service Ready for Deployment
items.summary: Amazon has enough Leo satellites to launch its broadband service, though its constellation remains significantly smaller than Starlink's.

items.title: Blue Origin's New Moon Lander Passes Crucial NASA Test
items.summary: Blue Origin's MK1 lander successfully survived a brutal dress rehearsal for NASA's Moon missions, marking a key milestone for future space travel.

items.title: X (Elon Musk's Platform) Returns to Brazil After Suspension
items.summary: The social media platform X is back in Brazil after more than a month of inaccessibility, having complied with all judicial demands following a clash with a Brazilian justice.

items.title: Spotify Launches Free Streaming Option in South Korea
items.summary: Spotify has introduced a free streaming tier in South Korea, expanding its service offerings in the region.

items.title: Cloudflare to Filter Web Crawlers Serving AI Companies
items.summary: Cloudflare announced it will block web crawlers used by AI companies, giving websites more control over how their content is utilized.

items.title: Google Loses Final Appeal Over $4.7B EU Android Antitrust Fine
items.summary: Europe's highest court upheld a record $4.7 billion fine imposed on Google in 2018 for Android antitrust violations, ending the company's final appeal.
